title: Trichilia cuneifolia
intro: Trichilia cuneifolia, also known as the Cut-Leaf Calabash Tree, is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, native to tropical Africa. The leaves and bark of Trichilia cuneifolia have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trichilia cuneifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, with the leaves and bark being used separately or in combination to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Trichilia cuneifolia is classified within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family includes over 300 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Trichilia cuneifolia is a species of plant native to South America, specifically in the countries of Argentina, Bolivia, and Paraguay. The genus Trichilia is comprised of approximately 130 species, many of which are found in Central and South America. The classification of Trichilia cuneifolia reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history within the Meliaceae family.
